A test case asserting that the negation of false is strictly equal to true.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the logical negation of `false` in JavaScript. The expression `!false` evaluates to `true`, and the assertion `assert(!false === true)` verifies this by comparing the result to `true`. This code provides narrow functionality, serving as a basic test to confirm the expected behavior of the logical NOT operator in JavaScript. It is a single-line script that demonstrates a fundamental concept in logical operations.
